questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The circuit shown in figure consisting of three identical lamps and two coils is connected to a direct current source. The ohmic resistance of the inducting coils is negligible. The switch is closed for a long time and then opened. Which of the following statements is/are correct for the instant immediately after opening the switch?
options: All the lamps are turned off
Brightness of $B_2 \& B_3$ remains unchanged
Brightness of $\mathrm{B}_1$ suddenly decreases.
Brightness of $B_1$ suddenly increases
questions.solution: Due to self induced emf current through $\mathrm{B}_2$ and $\mathrm{B}_3$ remains unchanged but current through $\mathrm{B}_1$ increased.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A conducting rod PQ of length $\ell$ is dogged with a constant force F along two smooth parallel rails separated by a distance $\ell$ as shown in the adjacent figure. Then choose the correct statement(s).
options: terminal velocity of the rod, $v_t=\frac{2 F R}{B^2 \ell^2}$
terminal velocity of the rod, $v_t=\frac{F R}{B^2 \ell^2}$
maximum charge on the capacitor, $q_{\max }=\frac{F C R}{B \ell}$
maximum charge on the capacitor, $q_{\max }=\frac{2 F C R}{B \ell}$
questions.solution: In steady state $B \ell V_{\text {terminal }}=\frac{q_{\max }}{C}$ also $\mathrm{iB} \ell=\mathrm{F} \& \mathrm{i}=\frac{B \ell v_{\text {terminal }}}{R}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A frame a bed and a sliding rod PQ of resistance R, start moving with the velocities v and 2 v respectively parallel to a long wire carrying steady current I, as shown in the figure.
options: $\text { charge on the capacitor at time } t \text { is } q=\frac{C \mu_0 i v \ln 2}{2 \pi}\left[1-e^{-U / R C}\right]$
$\text { charge on the capacitor at time } t \text { is } q=\frac{\mathrm{C} \mu_0 \mathrm{iv} \ln 2}{\pi}\left[1-\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{t} / \mathrm{Rc}}\right]$
$\text { current passing through the resistor at time } t \text { is } i=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{ivin} 2}{2 \pi R}\left[e^{-U R C}\right]$
$\text { current passing through the capacitor at time } \mathrm{t} \text { is } \mathrm{i}=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{iv} \ln 2}{\pi \mathrm{R}}\left[\mathrm{e}^{-t / R C}\right]$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \varepsilon_{P C}=\frac{v \mu_0 i}{2 \pi} \ln 2 \\ & \varepsilon_{P Q}=\frac{2 v_{\mu_0} i}{2 \pi} \ln 2 \\ & \varepsilon_{\text {net }}=\frac{\mu_0 i v}{2 \pi} \ln 2 \\ & q=C \varepsilon\left[1-e^{-t R C}\right] \\ & i=\frac{\varepsilon}{R} e^{-t / R C}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: A conducting wire of length $\ell$ and mass m can slide without friction on two parallel rails and is connected to capacitance C. Whole system lies in a magnetic field B and a constant force F is applied to the rod. Then
options: $\text { the rod moves with constant velocity }$
$\text { the rod moves with an acceleration of } \frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{~m}+\mathrm{B}^2 \ell^2 \mathrm{c}}$
$\text { there is constant charge on the capacitor. }$
$\text { charge on the capacitor increases with time }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Inside a very long solenoid of radius R, consider the triangular circuit $A B C$ as shown in figure. Axis of solenoid is perpendicular to the plane of paper. If the magnetic field inside the solenoid changes at the rate $\mathrm{dB} / \mathrm{dt}$, then the magnitude of induced emf $(A B=B C$ and $A C=2 R)$
options: in the triangular circuit $A B C$ is $R^2\left|\frac{d B}{d t}\right|$
in the triangular circuit $A B C$ is $\frac{R^2}{2}\left|\frac{d B}{d t}\right|$
between the ends of wire $A B$ is $\frac{R^2}{2}\left|\frac{d B}{d t}\right|$, if $A C$ and $B C$ were removed from the circuit
between the ends of wire $B C$ is $\frac{R^2}{2}\left|\frac{d B}{d t}\right|$, if $A C$ and $A B$ were removed from the circuit,
questions.solution:
Faraday's law induced e.m.f. $=-$ rate of change of magnetic flux Inside the solenoid, flux $=\mathrm{B} \times$ Area where $B$ is the magnetic flux density Induced e.m.f. $=-\frac{d}{d t}(B \times$ Area $)=-$ Area $\times \frac{d B}{d t}=-R^2 \frac{d B}{d t}$ since area of triangle $A B C$ is $R^2$. (2) the important point is that a changing magnetic field induces a circulating electric field (from which we derive the induced e.m.f.). in other words, there are no radial components of electric field (radiating outwards or inwards) so that no e.m.f is actually induced in the length $A C$ of the circuit.
Hence the induced e.m. $f$ is shared out between $A B$ and $B C$. So for $A B$, induced e.m.f. $=-\frac{1}{2} R^2 \frac{d B}{d t}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
